quantum_computing.title: Introduction to Quantum Computing
quantum_computing.snippet: Quantum computing harnesses quantum-mechanical phenomena such as superposition and entanglement to process information. Unlike classical bits, quantum bits (qubits) can represent 0 and 1 simultaneously, enabling massive parallelism for certain problem classes.

quantum_computing.title: Quantum Advantage and Current Hardware
quantum_computing.snippet: Recent experiments have demonstrated quantum advantage on specific tasks using superconducting qubit processors. Error correction remains the primary engineering challenge before fault-tolerant quantum computing becomes practical.

container_orchestration_kubernetes.title: Kubernetes Architecture Deep Dive
container_orchestration_kubernetes.snippet: Kubernetes provides declarative container orchestration with built-in service discovery, load balancing, and self-healing. Its control plane manages scheduling across a cluster of worker nodes.

container_orchestration_kubernetes.title: Kubernetes vs Nomad: A Practitioner's Comparison
container_orchestration_kubernetes.snippet: While Kubernetes dominates the container orchestration market, HashiCorp Nomad offers a simpler operational model for mixed workloads including containers, VMs, and standalone binaries.

container_orchestration_nomad.title: HashiCorp Nomad: Flexible Workload Orchestration
container_orchestration_nomad.snippet: Nomad is a single-binary orchestrator that schedules containers, Java apps, and batch jobs. It integrates with Consul for service discovery and Vault for secrets management.

container_orchestration_swarm.title: Docker Swarm Mode Overview
container_orchestration_swarm.snippet: Docker Swarm mode provides native clustering for Docker engines. While simpler to set up than Kubernetes, its feature set is narrower and the project receives minimal active development.
